stories: Good morning Sean, it's Saturday, the twenty-fifth of April, 2026
Here's a quietly lovely one to start with
When Apollo 16 astronaut Charlie Duke walked on the Moon in 1972, he left behind a small family photo of himself, his wife, and their two young sons, sealed in a plastic wrapper
It's still sitting there on the lunar surface today, more than fifty years on, weathering nothing but micrometeorites and sunlight
There's something quite humbling about a private gesture preserved that perfectly, that far from home
Looking at your day, your Saskatoon Saturday is wide open
The calendar is clear, the Friday drinks at Cathedral Social Hall are wrapped, and you've got a proper weekend ahead
Today's also Anzac Day back home in Australia, so a moment of quiet acknowledgement if you're inclined
Good morning Sean, it's Friday, the twenty-fourth of April, 2026
Here's a strange one to kick things off: the humble wax worm, the caterpillar of the wax moth that beekeepers curse for chewing through hives, can actually digest plastic
Its saliva contains enzymes that chemically break down polyethylene, the world's most common plastic, in a matter of hours
Researchers are chasing those enzymes as a potential industrial recycling tool
Bit humbling to realise a creature you'd squash without thinking might hold one answer to the plastic problem
Looking at your day, it's a fairly focused one
You've got the Weekly Planning and Look-Ahead with Bob and Rehan from ten to eleven on Webex, your lunch hold at twelve, and then a routine catch-up with Pat from two to two-thirty, also on Webex
Decent runway either side of the Pat slot for heads-down work before the week wraps
Good morning Sean, it's Thursday, the twenty-third of April, 2026
Under that dense white fur, their skin is jet black
The hairs themselves are hollow and translucent, scattering light in a way that just reads as white to our eyes, and the dark skin underneath helps soak up any sunlight that makes it through
Evolution doing some pretty clever engineering
Looking at your day, it's a fairly light one
You've got the Weekly Planning and Look-Ahead Session with Bob and Rehan at ten on Webex, which should wrap by eleven
Then your lunch hold is locked in from twelve to one
That leaves you a solid stretch of open afternoon to push on whatever needs your focus this week
